body.description: Tumor volume doubling time (TVDT) of untreated pancreatic adenocarcinoma measured from serial helical CT (clinical natural history; no anticancer therapy during observation).

inputs.name: tvd_mean
inputs.description: Reported mean tumor volume doubling time (TVDT) for primary lesions
inputs.value_snippet: "The mean TVDT of the nine primary lesions of pancreatic carcinoma was 159 ± 67 days"

inputs.name: tvd_sd
inputs.value_snippet: "The mean TVDT of the nine primary lesions of pancreatic carcinoma was 159 ± 67 days"

inputs.name: n_mc_samples
inputs.description: Monte Carlo sample size for parametric bootstrap of the observable distribution
inputs.value_snippet: Set by calibration pipeline (not from paper).

calibration_target_estimates.distribution_code:
def derive_distribution(inputs, ureg):
    import numpy as np

    # Inputs (Pint Quantities)
    mean = inputs['tvd_mean']
    sd = inputs['tvd_sd']
    n = int(inputs['n_mc_samples'].to('dimensionless').magnitude)

    # Parametric bootstrap: assume the reported mean±SD describes an underlying normal distribution
    # for TVDT across lesions, with physiologic truncation at >0.
    samples = np.random.normal(loc=mean.magnitude, scale=sd.magnitude, size=n) * mean.units
    samples = samples[samples.magnitude > 0]

    median_obs = np.median(samples.magnitude) * mean.units

    q25 = np.percentile(samples.magnitude, 25) * mean.units
    q75 = np.percentile(samples.magnitude, 75) * mean.units
    iqr_obs = (q75 - q25)

    ci95_obs = [
        np.percentile(samples.magnitude, 2.5) * mean.units,
        np.percentile(samples.magnitude, 97.5) * mean.units,
    ]

    return {
        'median_obs': median_obs.to('day'),
        'iqr_obs': iqr_obs.to('day'),
        'ci95_obs': [ci95_obs[0].to('day'), ci95_obs[1].to('day')],
    }

scenario.description: Untreated natural-history observation: primary pancreatic tumor followed by serial helical CT; tumor size on CT used to compute tumor volume doubling time (TVDT).

measurements.computation_code:
def compute_measurement(time, species_dict, ureg):
    # Model-derived analogue of clinical TVDT for the primary tumor.
    # Compute instantaneous exponential growth rate of tumor burden at the measurement time,
    # then convert to doubling time.
    #
    # NOTE: This requires that the simulator provide (or that the pipeline can compute)
    # d(V_T.C1)/dt at 'time'. If only discrete outputs are available, the pipeline should
    # compute a local derivative around 'time'.

    tumor = species_dict['V_T.C1']  # tumor cells
    dtumor_dt = species_dict['d_dt__V_T.C1']  # tumor cells/day (expected to be provided by pipeline)

    k = dtumor_dt / tumor  # 1/day
    ln2 = ureg.Quantity(2.0).to('dimensionless')
    ln2 = ureg.Quantity(__import__('math').log(ln2.magnitude), 'dimensionless')

    tvdt = (ln2 / k).to('day')
    return tvdt

body.study_overview: The study quantified the natural growth rate of pancreatic adenocarcinoma by calculating tumor volume doubling time (TVDT) from serial helical CT scans in patients who received no anticancer treatment during the observation period.
body.study_design: Nine patients with pancreatic carcinoma underwent serial helical CT imaging; tumor size on CT was measured and used to calculate TVDT for the primary lesions, then related to survival.
body.derivation_explanation: We treat the reported mean±SD TVDT as defining an underlying (approximately) normal distribution across lesions, then use a Monte Carlo parametric bootstrap to generate a distribution of TVDT values (truncated to >0 days). The reported range is stored as additional extracted context but is not used to parameterize the distribution because the paper does not report a distribution form conditioned on the range.

key_assumptions.text: The paper’s reported mean ± SD for TVDT can be approximated by a normal distribution across lesions/patients, with truncation to enforce positive doubling times.
Although the paper states 'pancreatic carcinoma/adenocarcinoma', we map this to PDAC for model calibration; histologic subtypes are not fully specified in the accessible abstract.
The model-computed observable uses an instantaneous growth-rate-to-doubling-time conversion at a reference timepoint; clinically, TVDT was computed from serial CT measurements over an interval.
Disease stage (locally advanced vs metastatic) is not specified in the accessible abstract; we label the intended model-matching stage as locally_advanced, but this is a context mismatch risk.
Because the provided system enum does not include 'clinical.imaging', we encode system as 'clinical.biopsy' while explicitly noting the true measurement modality was helical CT imaging.

body.key_study_limitations: Small sample size (n=9) and limited accessible methodological detail from the abstract (e.g., exact CT follow-up intervals, staging breakdown, and volumetry method). Stage/burden and strict treatment-naïve status cannot be fully verified from the abstract alone, increasing context-mismatch uncertainty.

primary_data_source.title: Growth rate of pancreatic adenocarcinoma: initial clinical experience

secondary_data_sources.title: Calibration pipeline default for Monte Carlo sample size

body.description: Fraction of intratumoral CD4+ T cells that are regulatory T cells (Tregs; CD4+CD25+FoxP3+) in human PDAC, measured by flow cytometry on fresh tumor samples collected at surgical resection.

inputs.name: treg_fraction_mean
inputs.description: Mean fraction of CD4+ T cells that are Tregs in PDAC tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), computed from reported 17.8%.
inputs.value_snippet: “We also observed that the proportions of CD4+ T cells (PDA-TIL vs. C-PT: 59.47±0.5974% vs. 12.6±1.343%) and Tregs (PDA-TIL vs. C-PT: 17.8±0.7075% vs. 0.01±0.005%) in TILs were markedly increased compared to C-PTLs (both P<0.001) ( Figure 1B, 1C and 1D ).”

inputs.name: treg_fraction_sd_assumed
inputs.description: Uncertainty on Treg fraction taken directly from the reported “±0.7075%” as an SD-equivalent on the percent scale, converted to fraction (0.7075% -> 0.007075). The paper does not explicitly state whether ± is SD or SEM.

inputs.name: lower_bound_fraction
inputs.description: Lower truncation bound for a biological fraction.

inputs.name: upper_bound_fraction
inputs.description: Upper truncation bound for a biological fraction.

inputs.name: n_mc_samples
inputs.description: Monte Carlo sample size for parametric bootstrap.

calibration_target_estimates.distribution_code:
def derive_distribution(inputs, ureg):
    import numpy as np

    mean = inputs['treg_fraction_mean'].to(ureg.dimensionless)
    sd = inputs['treg_fraction_sd_assumed'].to(ureg.dimensionless)
    n = int(inputs['n_mc_samples'].to(ureg.dimensionless).magnitude)

    lb = inputs['lower_bound_fraction'].to(ureg.dimensionless).magnitude
    ub = inputs['upper_bound_fraction'].to(ureg.dimensionless).magnitude

    # Parametric bootstrap: sample a Normal(mean, sd) and truncate to [0, 1]
    samples = np.random.normal(loc=mean.magnitude, scale=sd.magnitude, size=n)
    samples = np.clip(samples, lb, ub) * ureg.dimensionless

    median_obs = np.median(samples.magnitude) * ureg.dimensionless
    q25 = np.percentile(samples.magnitude, 25) * ureg.dimensionless
    q75 = np.percentile(samples.magnitude, 75) * ureg.dimensionless
    iqr_obs = (q75 - q25).to(ureg.dimensionless)
    ci95_obs = [np.percentile(samples.magnitude, 2.5) * ureg.dimensionless,
               np.percentile(samples.magnitude, 97.5) * ureg.dimensionless]

    return {'median_obs': median_obs, 'iqr_obs': iqr_obs, 'ci95_obs': ci95_obs}

scenario.description: Treatment-naive human PDAC patients undergoing surgical resection; fresh tumor tissue obtained intraoperatively, digested to single-cell suspensions, and analyzed by flow cytometry to quantify CD4+ Tregs among CD4+ T cells in tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs).

measurements.computation_code:
def compute_measurement(time, species_dict, ureg):
    # Fraction of CD4+ T cells that are Tregs in tumor
    # Model mapping assumption:
    #   CD4 pool in tumor = Th + Th_exh + Treg
    treg = species_dict['V_T.Treg']
    th = species_dict['V_T.Th']
    th_exh = species_dict['V_T.Th_exh']
    cd4_total = treg + th + th_exh
    frac = treg / cd4_total
    return frac.to(ureg.dimensionless)

body.study_overview: The study quantified immunosuppressive regulatory T cells within the PDAC tumor microenvironment and related intratumoral immune composition to clinicopathologic features and prognosis.
body.study_design: Fresh tumor samples collected during surgery were enzymatically dissociated to isolate tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes, then stained for CD3/CD4/CD25/FoxP3 and analyzed by flow cytometry; Tregs were identified as FoxP3+CD25+ cells within the CD4+ gate.
body.derivation_explanation:
1) Extracted the reported PDA tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte (PDA-TIL) Treg percentage from the Results/Figure 1D: 17.8±0.7075%.
2) Interpreted the reported Treg percentage as the percentage of Tregs among CD4+ T cells, based on the Figure 1 caption explicitly stating Tregs were gated on CD4+ T cells.
3) Converted percent to fraction: 17.8% -> 0.178.
4) Converted the reported ±0.7075% to a fraction uncertainty 0.007075; because the paper does not specify whether the ± is SD or SEM, the extraction treats it as an SD-equivalent for calibration and flags this as an assumption.
5) Distribution is represented by a truncated Normal on [0,1] and summarized via Monte Carlo bootstrap to obtain median, IQR, and 95% interval.

key_assumptions.text: The reported value “17.8±0.7075%” for PDA-TIL Tregs is interpreted as the percentage of Tregs among CD4+ T cells (not among all CD3+ T cells), supported by the Figure 1 legend stating Tregs were defined “based on the gating of CD4+ T cells.”

The uncertainty term “±0.7075%” is treated as an SD-equivalent (rather than SEM), because the paper does not explicitly specify SD vs SEM. This affects the width of the calibration target distribution.

Measurement timepoint is treated as day 0 (time of surgical resection/tissue acquisition), since samples are described as collected during surgery and processed ex vivo without an additional reported delay.
Model mapping assumes tumor CD4+ T cells correspond to (Th + Th_exh + Treg) and the observable is Treg / (Th + Th_exh + Treg).

body.key_study_limitations: The FCM subset (n=45) appears to come from a broader surgical cohort and may include a mix of TNM stages (Table 1 includes stage III+IV and some M1 cases), so it is not a perfectly isolated ‘resectable, stage I/II only’ estimate. Additionally, the manuscript reports values with “±” but does not clearly define whether these are SD or SEM, introducing ambiguity in uncertainty quantification.

primary_data_source.title: An Increased Abundance of Tumor-Infiltrating Regulatory T Cells Is Correlated with the Progression and Prognosis of Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma
